description: WiringBench (B4): corpus-scale edge adjudication over corpus v2 under C3 tier-1 defaults. The reproducible stratified sample is results/semantics-corpus-sample.json; the adjudication protocol is tests/benchmarks/adjudication-protocol.md.

adjudication.method: bounded strong-model spot-check of the wiring types (the moat); the full 456-edge per-type adjudication is the pre-registered bounded human/strong-model pass and remains a follow-up

spotCheck.note: 21 wiring edges inspected against source per the protocol; all read as correct (no false positives), consistent with Suite A's 23/23 on the OrderingApp fixture. Coarse target rendering on route_handles (a truncated method name, for example 'Program.') does not make the route-to-handler edge wrong.

coChangeDecision.record: D6 stays held. The recorded co-change A/B (ranking.json) is default MRR 0.197 versus default-without-co-change 0.208, recall@10 flat - within CI on a mostly-syntax ranking corpus. B4 intended to re-adjudicate the prior on a SEMANTIC-mode corpus, but the ranking suite scores over the retired dotnet-prs-v1 dataset (changed-file ground truth); corpus v2 has no ranking PR set, so a semantic-mode co-change A/B is not runnable here (the same corpus-PR gap that bounds review/localize/ranking on corpus v2). The prior is held on the recorded within-CI evidence rather than flipped on noise, exactly as D6 requires. Flipping or a semantic-mode re-run awaits a corpus-v2 ranking PR set.

notes: Sample produced by `fuse eval semantics --corpus-sample 40 --manifest tests/benchmarks/corpus-v2.json --corpus D:/fuse-work/bench --restore` on the runner.

18 edge types appear across corpus v2 under C3 tier-1 defaults, up from 3 types on the retired mostly-syntax corpus - the semantic-mode corpus is what makes corpus-scale wiring adjudication meaningful (the B4 premise).
First-run deliverable is the reproducible stratified sample and its distribution; the full per-type precision table is the bounded adjudication follow-up (no pass bar on the first run, per the gate).
